commits.messageBody:
Records the deep-module decision that webfetch quietly returns
site-optimized representations per site type, with GitHub repository
orientation as the first instance beyond raw-file and issue/PR handling.

Co-Authored-By: Fixture Bot <bot@example.invalid>
commits.messageHeadline: Add ADR-0004: webfetch returns site-optimized representations

commits.messageBody:
Pointing webfetch at a bare github.com/{owner}/{repo} root now returns a
token-efficient, backbone-first orientation (owner/name, description,
default_branch, language/topics, homepage, depth-1 tree, README) instead
of the raw README, so an agent can plan follow-up reads without guessing
the default branch or top-level paths. Falls back to the prior README/HTML
behavior on any API failure and surfaces a clear rate-limit message.

Implements ADR-0004.

Co-Authored-By: Fixture Bot <bot@example.invalid>
commits.messageHeadline: Return orientation for GitHub repository root URLs

commits.messageBody:
The orientation README was fetched anonymously from raw.githubusercontent
while metadata and tree used githubApiHeaders, so a private repo's README
was unreadable even with a token set. Fetch it via GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/readme
with the shared auth headers and Accept: application/vnd.github.raw, which
also resolves non-README.md filenames. Addresses codex review on PR #63.

Co-Authored-By: Fixture Bot <bot@example.invalid>
commits.messageHeadline: Authenticate orientation README fetch via REST readme endpoint

statusCheckRollup.name: Node lint, format, typecheck, and unit tests

title: Return orientation for GitHub repository root URLs
